data.title: Applied Digital stock surges to $34.20 in after-hours trading, revenue up 84% to $64.2 million, $11 billion lease deal signed.
data.description: Applied Digital Corporation's stock price surged to $34.20 in after-hours trading, a 16.76% increase from the regular session close of $29.29, following the release of their fiscal first-quarter 2026 earnings report. The report showed a significant year-over-year revenue increase of 84%, totaling $64.2 million compared to $34.8 million the previous year. Despite the growth, the company reported a net loss of $27.8 million, or $0.11 per share, with an adjusted loss of $0.03 per share and adjusted EBITDA of $500,000. Additionally, Applied Digital secured a lease deal with CoreWeave for 150 megawatts at its North Dakota facility, bringing the total contracted revenue to $11 billion. Applied Digital's stock is currently trending on the NASDAQ.

data.title: VILVI Group's consolidated sales for September 2025 reached EUR 24.29 million, up by 6.5% from 2024. The Group's sales for Jan-Sept 2025 totaled EUR 215.82 million, a 19.0% increase from the previous year.
data.description: In September 2025, the VILVI Group, composed of Vilkyškių pieninė AB, Modest AB, Kelmės pieninė AB, Kelmės pienas UAB, Pieno logistika AB, and Baltic Dairy Board SIA, reported consolidated sales of EUR 24.29 million, representing a 6.5% rise from September 2024. For the period of January to September 2025, the Group recorded consolidated sales totaling EUR 215.82 million, marking a significant 19.0% increase compared to the same period in the previous year.

data.title: Millions watch Wuqiao Circus Festival with 24-hour global livestream from Cangzhou, China, on multiple platforms.
data.description: In Cangzhou, China, on October 10, 2025, the 20th China Wuqiao International Circus Festival took place from September 28 to 30 and was broadcast live for a cumulative 24 hours across various platforms. The event was streamed on the Xinhuanet App, Enjoy Hebei Facebook account, and Cangzhou YouTube, TikTok, and Instagram accounts.

data.title: BNB Chain memecoins plummet by over 30%, raising questions about the end of “Meme Rush” by Binance.
data.description: The value of BNB Chain's memecoin experienced a sharp decline following the introduction of "Meme Rush" by Binance. This move revealed liquidity shortages and the potential risks associated with concentrated wallets. Many are now questioning if the era of memecoins is coming to an end after this development.

data.title: PST Group AB: Notifications from UAB Prosperus Group, Artea Baltic Fund, and Clairmont Holdings Ltd of loss of voting rights.
data.description: PST Group AB has been informed by UAB Prosperus Group, Artea Baltic Fund managed by UAB “Artea Asset Management,” and Clairmont Holdings Ltd about the withdrawal of voting rights. The specifics are detailed in the notifications received.

data.title: Yamato Transport, Mitsubishi Fuso, and TIER IV partner with Japan govt for autonomous trucking project.
data.description: On October 10, 2025, Yamato Transport Co., Ltd., based in Chuo-ku, Tokyo, and led by President Seiichi Awa, announced a collaboration with Mitsubishi Fuso Truck and Bus Corporation, headquartered in Kawasaki City, Kanagawa Prefecture, and led by President and CEO Karl Deppen. This partnership aims to enhance transportation services. The two companies will invest a substantial amount in this venture, totaling a significant monetary figure.

data.description: SKF, based in Gothenburg, Sweden, has announced that their Q3 results for 2025 will be released on October 29th at around 07:30 CET. data.title: 'Bitcoin Jesus' Roger Ver nears agreement with Justice Department in tax evasion case; may resolve $48 million fraud charges.
data.description: Renowned Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) investor Roger Ver has reportedly reached a preliminary agreement with the Justice Department to settle a criminal tax fraud case against him. The agreement, which has yet to be officially filed with the court and could be subject to modifications, may lead to the charges being dismissed if Ver adheres to the terms. Sources familiar with the matter, cited by The New York Times, suggest that if Ver fails to comply with the agreement, he could face significant consequences. Ver, also known as "Bitcoin Jesus," faced indictments last year for allegedly evading taxes and committing fraud by failing to pay $48 million in taxes. For further details, the complete story is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Analyst predicts Bitcoin bottom at $110K, dismisses $95K possibility, warns rally could end if it drops.
data.description: Analyst James Check believes Bitcoin is unlikely to drop to $95,000 and suggests that if it does, the current rally may come to a halt for an extended period.

data.title: Group urges Ireland to abandon work on encryption ‘backdoor law’. The bill in development must be scrapped, says Global Encryption Coalition.
data.description: The development of the Irish Communications Interception and Lawful Access Bill is still ongoing, and drafting has not started yet. However, the Global Encryption Coalition is urging for its immediate cancellation.
Bekaert provides update on share buyback program and liquidity agreement.

data.title: Metals at record highs in 2025, leading investors to shift to Bitcoin. BTC expected to rally in Q4 due to undervaluation.
data.description: In 2025, the prices of precious metals reached unprecedented levels, establishing new record highs. This surge in prices has resulted in Bitcoin being perceived as relatively undervalued. As a result, Bitcoin (BTC) is poised for a robust rally in the fourth quarter of the year. The strong performance of precious metals has drawn attention to the potential for significant gains in the cryptocurrency market, particularly for Bitcoin. Investors are closely watching the market as BTC shows promising indicators for a significant upward movement in the near future.

data.title: IDEX, Hitachi, Airtel launch India's 1st RuPay biometric payment card at Global Fintech Fest, marking secure digital payment milestone.
data.description: IDEX Biometrics has introduced India's inaugural RuPay biometric payment card in partnership with Hitachi Payment Services and Airtel Payments Bank. The card was unveiled at the renowned Global Fintech Fest in Mumbai, representing a major advancement in safe and accessible digital payments in India. This collaboration brings cutting-edge technology to the forefront, enhancing security and convenience for users across the country.

data.title: Fed's Barr predicts inflation may persist till 2027, hints at prolonged high rates. Inflation above 2% driven by 11% spike in tariffs.
data.description: Federal Reserve Governor Michael Barr cautioned about a prolonged battle against inflation, indicating that investors hoping for rate cuts may need to be patient. Speaking at the Economic Club of Minnesota Luncheon, Barr projected that it could be as late as the end of 2027 before price increases align with the central bank's 2% target. Barr highlighted that the median FOMC participant anticipates headline PCE inflation will not reach the 2% goal until late 2027, marking the lengthiest period of PCE inflation above 2% since a seven-year period ending in 1993. He attributed this trend partly to the significant increase in tariffs this year. In a separate perspective, Fed Governor Stephen Miran dismissed concerns about tariff-induced inflation, stating it is not a significant factor. The inflation rate soared this year, reaching approximately 11 percent as noted by Barr.
